factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields surged on Friday following stronger-than-expected May NFP report (172K vs 85K expected); 10-year yields rose ~6 bps and 2-year yields rose >12 bps. Higher nominal yields with sticky inflation expectations push real yields higher, which is deeply bearish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY rallied to near 100.10 on Friday from 99.16 lows following the strong jobs report. Stronger USD creates direct headwind for XAUUSD as gold becomes more expensive in foreign currency terms; strong dollar diverts safe-haven flows away from bullion.

factors.name: Federal Reserve policy expectations
factors.summary: Bond market pricing now reflects expectations of a 25 bps rate hike in 2026 (per KPMG Chief Economist Diane Swonk); strong jobs data has pushed rate-cut expectations further out of reach. Fed Chair Warsh now faces policy tests with hawkish sentiment entrenched, opposite of a dovish pivot.

factors.name: US economic data (NFP)
factors.summary: Nonfarm Payrolls came in at 172K for May vs 85K expected—a strong surprise that eliminated near-term recession fears and reinforced expectations for higher rates, not cuts. This is the dominant macro catalyst of the period.

factors.name: Inflation expectations
factors.summary: While the news feed does not show latest CPI or PCE print, the strong jobs report combined with persistent service-sector inflation cited by KPMG keeps inflation expectations sticky. No evidence of disinflation that would support lower real yields.

factors.name: Risk sentiment regime
factors.summary: Equity selloff underway: S&P 500 down 2.65%, NASDAQ down 4.18% (worst day since April 2025), with $1.8 trillion wipeout. While equities weakness can support safe-haven gold, the decline is driven by higher rates and USD strength—not the conditions that drive gold as a hedge. Bitcoin crashed to lowest since Oct 2024 below $60K, signaling broad de-risking away from 'risk' assets and towards USD/cash.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions
factors.summary: US strikes Iranian radar sites in latest Gulf flare-up; Ukraine war persists with Russian attacks on Kherson; Israel-Hezbollah/Lebanon tensions ongoing. While geopolitical risk is elevated, it is not driving gold currently—USD strength and rate expectations dominate.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying
factors.summary: No fresh news on PBoC, RBI, or other structural CB gold demand in the past 24h. This structural bid remains a multi-month/multi-year factor but does not offset the immediate 24h headwinds.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows
factors.summary: No GLD or IAU holdings data in this news window; cannot assess institutional positioning changes in real time.

factors.name: Silver price action
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) crashed ~8% on Friday and is poised to end the week down ~10%, approaching the 200-day SMA near $67.79. Silver's sharper decline than gold reflects same driver: rate expectations and USD strength, with added pressure from risk-off liquidation.

key_levels.resistance: $4,400 (intraday high from rally off lows), $4,450 (multi-week resistance)

screening.narrative: Gold has entered a sharp downtrend following Friday's stronger-than-expected 172K NFP print, which reignited hawkish Fed expectations and triggered a broad bond and equity selloff. Real yields have moved sharply higher as 10-year nominal yields surged ~6 bps despite equity weakness, and the US Dollar rallied to 100.10—both powerful headwinds for XAUUSD. The move is underway (4h: -0.28%, 24h: -2.62%) but still in early Phase 2; the narrative is dominated by monetary policy normalization risk rather than geopolitical safe-haven demand, despite elevated Middle East tensions. Risk sentiment has deteriorated (equities down >2.5%, Bitcoin collapsing), but the direction of that risk-off flow is into USD and away from commodities, not into gold as a traditional hedge.
catalysts_ahead: No FOMC meeting imminent, but Chair Warsh commentary on rate policy likely to be heavily parsed
Next CPI/PCE print (likely mid-June) will be critical—any inflation surprise could flip the bias if downside, or deepen selling if upside
Geopolitical developments: Iran-US escalation, Ukraine war updates, Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire sustainability (low probability of driving gold higher given current rate regime)
Equity earnings reports continue this week; any disappointment could trigger further risk-off, but structure is into USD/rates, not commodities
Fed speakers and Powell commentary if any—critical for assessing rate path beyond 2026
screening.risk_warning: If upcoming CPI/PCE data comes in softer than expected OR if geopolitical escalation (Iran military action, major war development) forces a genuine safe-haven bid that overwhelms rate considerations, gold could reverse sharply higher. Additionally, if equity selloff triggers a 'flight to safety' that rotates into gold (rather than USD), the technicals could fail. Watch for retest of resistance around $4,400; if broken above on strong volume, Phase 2 thesis weakens.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2; do not chase the selloff. Wait for retest of $4,380–$4,400 resistance or a pullback from current lows to $4,320–$4,340 support before considering fresh shorts. A close below $4,300 on next bar would strengthen bearish conviction; conversely, a bounce and close back above $4,380 would signal phase exhaustion and warrant re-evaluation.

factors.name: US NFP / Labor Market
factors.summary: May NFP printed 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg, MarketWatch) — massive beat that sparked rate hike fears. KPMG Chief Economist notes bond market now pricing 25bp hike in 2026; Gene Sperling confirmed 'stronger than expected' print lowers recession risk but triggers hawkish Fed pivot.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Strong jobs data eliminated rate cut expectations entirely; Citigroup maintains lonely call for 3 cuts but market has repriced to rate HIKE probability. KPMG economist cited hawkish Fed sentiment driven by labor strength and persistent service inflation. Rate hike expectations are gold's nemesis.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields / Real Yields
factors.summary: FXStreet reports 'US Treasury yields skyrocket across whole curve' with 2-year up 12bp, 10-year up 6bp following NFP. Higher nominal yields imply sharply higher real yields (gold's #1 inverse correlation), making non-yielding gold less attractive.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 low (Investing.com) on NFP beat; NZD/USD down 3% weekly, PHP and KRW under pressure. Stronger dollar directly pressures gold pricing and reduces EM demand for dollar-denominated gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Selloff
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.18% (worst day since April 2025), S&P 500 -2.65%, $1.8 trillion wipeout (MarketWatch). Tech/momentum selling driven by yield spike; gold not acting as safe-haven but moving with risk-off in dollar-driven liquidation. Bitcoin cracked $60K lowest since Oct 2024.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: US attacked Iranian sites after Iran launched drones; Al Jazeera reports 'latest Gulf flare-up' with Kuwait/Bahrain under attack. Ongoing Russia-Ukraine exchanges (185 POWs each). Typically bullish for gold safe-haven bid, but being overwhelmed by dollar/yield surge.

factors.name: Inflation Trajectory
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE prints in window. Prior prints showed persistent service sector inflation (per KPMG). Strong labor market keeps inflationary pressure alive but actual inflation data not updated; wage growth lagging inflation per Gene Sperling commentary.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Positioning
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU flow data in news window. Absence of supportive flow data amid price decline suggests institutional positioning may be defensive or reducing.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh central bank purchase announcements in window. Structural bid from PBoC/RBI remains background support but not enough to offset macro headwinds.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: Iran-US tensions and Middle East flare-up should support oil (Iran threats to Red Sea choke point noted); inflationary for broader economy but not dominant driver for gold in current yield-driven regime.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD acting as primary safe-haven (up sharply); Bitcoin collapsing to $60K (down from highs, 'attention deficit' per MarketWatch). Gold losing to dollar in safe-haven competition; crypto weakness shows broader liquidation of non-yielding stores of value.

factors.name: Technical Price Action
factors.summary: Gold down -2.62% in 24h, already -0.28% in 4h; move in progress but not fully extended. Silver crashed toward 200-day SMA at $67.79, down 8% daily and 10% weekly (FXStreet), showing precious metals complex under broad pressure.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is under severe pressure following a blowout May NFP report (172K vs 85K expected) that has flipped Fed expectations from rate cuts to potential rate hikes in 2026. Treasury yields spiked across the curve (2Y +12bp, 10Y +6bp), pushing real yields sharply higher — gold's primary nemesis. The USD rallied hard to 100.10, creating a double headwind. While Middle East geopolitical tensions (US-Iran strikes, Gulf flare-up) would normally support gold's safe-haven bid, the macro regime shift to higher-for-longer (or higher-still) rates is overwhelming all other factors. The -2.62% 24h decline suggests the move is Phase 2 (in progress but not exhausted), with precious metals complex broadly selling off (silver -8% daily).
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers in coming days responding to NFP data (any hawkish confirmation extends selling)
June CPI print (next major inflation data point to confirm/deny rate hike path)
FOMC meeting later in June (market will watch for dot plot revision toward hikes)
Geopolitical developments in Iran-US conflict (could temporarily reverse dollar bid if major escalation)
Chinese economic data / PBoC policy (affects EM gold demand outlook)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed speakers push back against rate hike pricing, signaling data not strong enough to warrant tightening; (2) major geopolitical escalation (Iran closing Strait of Hormuz, broader Middle East war) overwhelms macro factors with safe-haven demand; (3) Treasury yields reverse sharply lower on risk-off flight to quality; (4) USD reverses below 99.00 on dovish Fed repricing. Gold reclaiming $4,400 would signal failed breakdown.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (underway, -2.62% in 24h) — avoid chasing here. Wait for retest of broken support at $4,380-$4,400 to enter shorts, or look for continuation sell signal below $4,300 after consolidation. Initial target $4,250 support zone.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields spiked sharply Friday on strong NFP (172K vs 85K expected per FXStreet). 10y Treasury yield surged ~6bp, 2y jumped 12bp+. Rising nominal yields with inflation data stable = rising real yields, which is highly bearish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD rallied to near 100.10 Friday after the hot jobs report (per FXStreet), following a daily low of 99.16. Stronger dollar directly pressures XAUUSD via inverse correlation. DXY strength is a primary headwind for gold.

factors.name: Fed Tightening Repricing
factors.summary: Bond markets now pricing 25bp Fed rate hike in 2026 per KPMG Chief Economist (Bloomberg). Market expectations shifted from potential cuts to hikes; hawkish pivot = gold-negative. Chair Warsh facing policy tests with cuts 'further out of reach' (Al Jazeera).

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (Equities Selloff)
factors.summary: S&P 500 down 2.65%, NASDAQ down 4.18% Friday (worst day since April 2025 per investingLive). Tech (Meta, Alphabet, Marvell, Micron) crushed on capex concerns and earnings pressure. Equity weakness typically doesn't boost safe-haven gold when driven by hawkish yields; instead, gold follows nominal yield direction.

factors.name: Inflation Data / Expectations
factors.summary: No major CPI/PCE surprise in this window; inflation expectations remain stable. The 172K NFP beat was the shock, signaling labor market strength and less recession risk—reducing safe-haven demand and reinforcing Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions (Iran-US)
factors.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes in Gulf; Iranian drones targeted US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain (BBC). This is a genuine near-term geopolitical tail risk that could spike oil and support gold as a hedge. However, no immediate escalation reported; situation appears contained for now.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No material news on PBoC, RBI, or other large central bank gold purchases in this 24h window. Structural bid remains but not a marginal driver over the 4h-24h horizon.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU flow data in news. Fund positioning likely adjusting downward on yield shock but no explicit data to confirm selling pressure.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Iran tensions could eventually lift oil and inflation expectations, but immediate impact unclear. Current move is yield-driven, not oil-driven.

screening.narrative: Gold surrendered 2.62% in 24h and 0.35% in 4h after a surprise strong US Nonfarm Payrolls (172K vs 85K expected) triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate hike expectations. Treasury yields surged across the curve, real yields rose, and USD rallied to 100.10, all primary headwinds for gold. The S&P 500 and NASDAQ crashed 2.65% and 4.18% respectively, but this equity selloff is not a classic safe-haven trigger—it is rate-driven capitulation. Geopolitical noise (US-Iran military exchanges) provides a modest tail-risk bid to gold, but the immediate momentum is bearish. Your prior SELL7/10 (Haiku) and BEARISH8/10 (Sonnet) reads remain intact; the bias has not shifted.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers / Powell testimony (if scheduled this week)
CPI/PCE inflation prints (if due before end of week)
Further Iran-US escalation or de-escalation signals
Tech earnings/capex guidance updates (Microsoft, Google, Meta)
DXY momentum continuation or reversal on technicals
Physical demand data (India, China)
Oil price spike if geopolitical tension escalates
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US tensions escalate sharply (military strike on major oil choke point or US military target), crude could spike 5-10%, reigniting inflation expectations and flipping gold BULLISH despite higher yields. Alternatively, if Fed speakers signal pause or dovish pivot, or if equity selling intensifies into a credit-stress event, safe-haven demand could override yield pressure and support gold. Watch 4,320 support; break below opens 4,240 target. Conversely, a retest of 4,380-4,420 on a small yield pullback would be a clear selling opportunity given the bearish macro setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 with 0.35% 4h decline underway. Wait for a 4h retest of $4,380–$4,400 before adding shorts; or initiate a small short near current levels with tight stop above $4,420. Avoid chasing the downside—pullback entry more prudent given yield volatility may trigger bounces.

factors.name: US NFP & Real Yields
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg/CNBC), massive beat. 10-year Treasury yields surged 6bps, 2-year up 12bps (FXStreet). TIPS real yields rising sharply - this is the single most correlated bearish factor for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Shift
factors.summary: KPMG Chief Economist Diane Swonk noted bond markets now pricing 25bps rate HIKE in 2026 (Bloomberg 22:57), complete reversal from cut expectations. Strong jobs data reinforces hawkish Fed sentiment, persistent service sector inflation cited.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied from 99.16 to near 100.10 post-NFP (FXStreet 19:21), NZD/USD down 3% weekly (FXStreet 18:35). Strong inverse correlation - DXY strength directly pressures gold.

factors.name: Risk-Off Equity Selloff
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.18%, worst day since April 2025, S&P 500 down 2.65% (Investing 20:40). Normally bullish for safe-haven gold, but yield spike and USD strength dominating. Gold acting as funding source in deleveraging, not safe-haven.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes in Gulf, US hit Iranian drones/radar, Tehran targeted US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain (BBC 06:16, Al Jazeera 00:00). Typically bullish but insufficient to override macro headwinds.

factors.name: Precious Metals Broader Selloff
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) crashed 8% daily, nearly 10% weekly, challenging 200-day SMA at $67.79 (FXStreet 20:08). Entire precious metals complex under severe pressure, confirming systematic selloff not isolated move.

factors.name: Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Market shifting from cut expectations to pricing rate hikes. Strong labor market (172K jobs vs 85K) removes any dovish Fed pivot rationale. Higher for longer now baseline scenario.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (Implied)
factors.summary: With gold down 2.62% in 24h and -0.35% in 4h, institutional positioning likely turning negative. No specific GLD/IAU data but price action suggests outflows accelerating.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven (USD)
factors.summary: USD acting as dominant safe-haven amid equity rout, not gold. Bitcoin also cracked $60K, lowest since Oct 2024 (CNBC 20:59), showing 'alternative gold' narratives failing.

factors.name: Treasury Curve Dynamics
factors.summary: Front-end yields rising faster (2Y +12bps vs 10Y +6bps) suggests Fed hike pricing, not just term premium. Flattening via front-end typically bearish for gold as opportunity cost rises.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is experiencing a systematic macro-driven selloff following the blockbuster NFP report (172K vs 85K expected). The combination of surging real yields, USD strength to 100.10, and markets now pricing a 2026 Fed rate HIKE instead of cuts creates a perfect bearish storm for gold. The move is Phase 2 (underway, -2.62% in 24h) but has further to run given the magnitude of the macro shift. Geopolitical risk premium from US-Iran Gulf strikes is being completely overridden by the yield/dollar dynamics. Silver's 10% weekly collapse confirms this is a precious metals washout, not isolated gold weakness.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (date TBD but likely June mid-month given current pricing)
Next CPI/PCE inflation print (critical to validate/refute hike expectations)
Fed speakers next week responding to NFP data
Escalation/de-escalation of US-Iran conflict
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed speakers push back hard against hike pricing, (2) DXY reverses below 99.00, (3) major geopolitical escalation (Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, direct US-Iran war declaration), (4) equity cascade forces coordinated central bank intervention. Gold reclaiming $4,400 would signal failed breakdown.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 and extending; best entry is short on any relief rally toward $4,380-$4,400 resistance, or add to shorts on break below $4,300 targeting $4,200 and potentially $4,000.
